Decision Details: Certain jurisdictions excluded and types of aid denied
Summary:
On 4/2, Missouri Gov. Mike Kehoe (R) requested Individual Assistance for 25 counties and Public Assistance for 20 counties.

On 5/21, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 18 counties (Dunklin, Laclede, Madison, New Madrid, Pemiscot, Pulaski, and Stoddard counties were excluded) and Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 20 counties.

On 6/2, FEMA denied Hazard Mitigation grants.

On 6/24, FEMA added 1 county for Public Assistance (categories A-G).

Type of Extreme Weather: Severe Storms, Straight-line Winds, Tornadoes, and Flooding
Decision Details: Certain jurisdictions excluded and types of aid denied
Summary:
On 4/1, Mississippi Gov. Tate Reeves (R) requested Individual Assistance for 14 counties and Public Assistance for 17 counties.

On 5/21, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 11 counties (Carroll, Holmes, and Jasper counties were excluded) and Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 17 counties.

On 6/2, FEMA denied Hazard Mitigation grants.

On 6/25, FEMA added 1 county for Public Assistance (categories A-G).

Decision Details: Certain types of aid denied
Summary:
On 3/21, Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt (R) requested Individual Assistance for 7 counties.

On 5/21, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 7 counties.

On 6/2, FEMA denied Hazard Mitigation grants.

Decision Details: Certain types of aid denied
Waiting Period: 48 days (from original request to partial approval)
Summary:
On 3/21, Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders (R) requested Individual Assistance for 9 counties and statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 3/29, Sanders amended her request to include Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 11 counties.

On 4/11, FEMA rejected Sanders' request, writing that "the damage from this event was not of such severity and magnitude as to be beyond the capabilities of the state, affected local governments, and voluntary agencies."

On 4/18, Sanders submitted an appeal. In it, she reiterated her request for Individual Assistance for 9 counties, Public Assistance for 11 counties, and statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 5/8, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 9 counties.

On 5/16, FEMA denied Hazard Mitigation grants.

Public Assistance appears to have been denied.

Type of Extreme Weather: Severe Storms, Straight-line Winds, Tornadoes, Flooding, Landslides, and Mudslides
Decision Details: Certain types of aid denied
Summary:
On 4/11, Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear (D) requested Individual Assistance for 13 counties, Public Assistance for 24 counties, and statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 4/24, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 13 counties.

On 4/28, Beshear added 16 counties to his request for Individual Assistance, expanded his request for Public Assistance to include an additional 66 counties, and asked again for statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 5/5, Beshear added 8 counties to his request for Individual Assistance.

On 5/14, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 24 additional counties.

FEMA has authorized Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 90 counties.

On 7/29, FEMA denied Hazard Mitigation grants.

Major Disaster Declaration Decision: Unclear (removed from FEMA daily operations briefing)
Summary: This request was included in FEMA's daily operations briefing before 5/29.

Waiting Period: 199 days from original request to appeal denial (81 days during Trump administration)

Decision Details: Certain types of aid denied
Summary:
After submitting his original request on 2/16, Virginia Gov. Glenn Youngkin (R) expanded it to include Individual Assistance and Public Assistance for additional jurisdictions, as well as statewide Hazard Mitigation. 

FEMA has authorized Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 35 counties and the City of Bristol.

On 5/23, FEMA denied Hazard Mitigation grants.

Individual Assistance appears to have been denied.

Type of Extreme Weather: Severe Storms, Straight-line Winds, Tornadoes, and Flooding
Decision Details: Certain types of aid denied
Summary:
Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt (R) requested Individual Assistance for an unspecified number of counties in December 2024 and Public Assistance for an unspecified number of counties in January 2025. Stitt also requested statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 3/18, FEMA authorized Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 8 counties and statewide Hazard Mitigation.

Individual Assistance appears to have been denied.

Type of Extreme Weather: Severe Storms, Straight-line Winds, Flooding, Landslides, and Mudslides
Decision Details: Certain jurisdictions excluded
Summary:
On 2/17, West Virginia Gov. Patrick Morrisey (R) requested Individual Assistance and Public Assistance for 13 counties, as well as statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 2/26, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 4 counties (McDowell, Mercer, Mingo and Wyoming), along with statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 3/10, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for Logan and Wayne counties.

On 3/19, FEMA authorized Public Assistance (categories A-G) for 10 counties (Greenbrier, Lincoln, Logan, McDowell, Mercer, Mingo, Monroe, Summers, Wayne, and Wyoming).

On 4/1, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for Raleigh County and Public Assistance (categories A-G) for Boone and Raleigh counties.

On 4/22, Morrisey said that he would consider appealing FEMA's denial of individual aid for Boone, Cabell, Greenbrier, Kanawha, Lincoln, Monroe, and Summers counties and public aid for Cabell and Kanawha counties.

Type of Extreme Weather: Severe Storms, Straight-line Winds, Flooding, Landslides, and Mudslides
Decision Details: Certain jurisdictions excluded
Summary:
After submitting his original request on 2/17, Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear (D) expanded it to include Individual Assistance for 22 counties, Public Assistance for 69 counties, and statewide Hazard Mitigation.

In its 2/24 declaration and subsequent amendments, FEMA authorized Individual Assistance for 16 counties, Public Assistance category B for 68 counties and categories A and C-G for 67 counties, and statewide Hazard Mitigation.

On 4/23, Beshear announced that he planned to appeal FEMA's 4/22 denial of individual aid for Butler, Franklin, Knox, Laurel, Lawrence, and Whitley counties and public aid for Simpson and Woodford counties.
